content: Just as human-language reflective learning in high school literacy curricula builds long-term critical thinking by asking learners to explicitly re-examine their own written outputs against different framing, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be improved by integrating graded, vocabulary-aligned reflective prompting schemas borrowed from structured language arts education. This turns abstract technical self-reflection into a teachable, iteratively improving skill for LLMs, similar to how students build reflective competence over a semester of coursework.
